title: Structured Consultation Note Task
instructions: Produce a neutral first-consultation support note aligned with Volume 2 Week 5: opening context, structured history, visual/reported concerns, treatment history, red flags or caution points, stated goals and underlying worries, and explicit doctor handover questions—no diagnostic labels, no good-candidate language unless policy explicitly allows, no sales-style closing.

rubric.label: Structure, neutrality, and handover usefulness
rubric.guidance: Assess Volume 2 Week 5 arc: headings, factual tone, goals versus fears, caution flags, and questions the clinician needs—not length for its own sake.

title: Screening Escalation Task
instructions: Document a screening scenario (real or realistic) per Volume 2 Week 6: classify as straightforward, cautionary, or high-priority for doctor review; state the escalation trigger in neutral language; list the next operational and clinical actions; explicitly avoid suitability confirmation or treatment promises.

rubric.label: Triage logic and documentation
rubric.guidance: Assess classification rationale, gap handling, escalation timing, and absence of candidacy claims from non-doctor staff.

title: Trust-Based Communication Roleplay
instructions: Draft a response to a hesitant patient demanding certainty before clinician review (Volume 2 Week 7): validate emotion, use measured expectation language, refuse absolute guarantees and graft or outcome promises, explain why doctor-led review matters, and offer to document concerns for handover.

rubric.label: Trust without false certainty
rubric.guidance: Assess empathy, restraint, scope-safe wording, and clear preservation of doctor authority.

title: Doctor Handover Note Task
instructions: Prepare a doctor-ready handover after a completed consultation (Volume 3 Week 9): objective narrative; described photographic evidence (standard views, consistency—no diagnostic labels); red flags or caution points; patient goals and worries; focused questions for the treating clinician. Keep it short enough to use in practice; avoid promotional adjectives and fact-dumping.

rubric.label: Handover quality and evidence alignment
rubric.guidance: Assess neutrality, structure, evidence integrity, doctor-focused questions, and scope-safe language consistent with the linked assessment and faculty documentation note.

title: High-Risk Consultation Response Task
instructions: Respond to a high-risk consultation scenario (Volume 3 Week 11 difficult strand): visible distress, dissatisfaction, or complaint pressure. Use non-defensive, boundary-safe language; refuse outcome guarantees; offer neutral documentation; state escalation to clinician or manager per protocol when complaint risk, complication concern, or severe distress applies.

rubric.label: Ethics, escalation, and communication maturity
rubric.guidance: Assess minimisation avoidance, scope clarity, realistic escalation plan, and protection from pressure selling.

title: Full Pathway Capstone Task
instructions: Complete the integrated capstone narrative (Volume 3 Week 12): thread first contact through documentation/handover, coordination touchpoints, follow-up judgement, one difficult interaction choice, and brief QA reflection—justify escalation versus watchful waiting at each fork in scope-safe, non-diagnostic language.

rubric.label: Integrated competency
rubric.guidance: Assess communication, documentation, escalation, coordination, and scope together; reject responses that smuggle clinical authority into support roles.
